2026-07-12.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function called offline_and_remove_memory_ranges() to the Linux kernel's memory hotplug module. The function takes an array of memory ranges and processes them as one operation under a single lock, ensuring atomicity and preventing race conditions between system daemons trying to manage the same resource. It first offlines every block in all ranges, then removes the ranges only if all are offline. If any offline step fails, the whole operation is reverted, leaving the device in a consistent state. This patch also updates the existing offline_and_remove_memory() function to use the new helper and move its logic into one place.
